rag_rules: Generate only syntactically valid AGREE clauses using visible symbols and case-provided relations, bounds, or constants.
Write contracts as visible input/output relationships in the current component scope; do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths unless they are explicitly exposed.
generic_rules: Prefer direct deterministic guarantees that relate outputs or helper equations to visible inputs and helper variables.
Introduce helper eq or const declarations only when they make an expression clearer, and ensure each helper has a type consistent with its expression.
Add range or envelope assumptions/guarantees only when concrete limits or named visible values are provided.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE forms exactly: assume/guarantee with labels, eq name : type = expression;, const name : type = value;, and assign output = expression; when appropriate.
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching, and use initialized temporal recurrence only as init -> pre(x) patterns when state is truly required.
Use AGREE operators and scalar types: and, or, not(...), =>, and types int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not invent hidden variables, thresholds, bounds, or domain facts that are not supplied by the case or visible model symbols.
Do not use AADL property names, unit tokens, or unresolved model metadata as AGREE expression variables.
Do not mismatch helper types with expression types; comparisons produce bool, integer arithmetic produces int, and real arithmetic produces real.
irrelevant_topics: Example-specific clamping/envelope implementation snippets that assume min/max variables not present in the current visible symbols.
